name: Betaine HCl
summary: Betaine HCl is an acidifying supplement intended to increase gastric acidity, but clinical outcome evidence is very limited.
description: Betaine HCl provides hydrochloride for gastric acid support and betaine as a methyl-donor; evidence is mainly physiologic rather than robust clinical outcome data.
safetyNotes: In a nine-person study, single betaine HCl doses up to 4,500 mg rapidly lowered post-meal gastric pH. Because gastric pH can change absorption of pH-sensitive medicines, medication-specific review is needed; repeated-use, ulcer, reflux, gastritis, pregnancy, breastfeeding, liver, and kidney safety were not established by this study.
